strengths: The commit message clearly indicates that it's a feature addition, which is great for tracking project progress.
The integrity score of 5/5 suggests that the code changes are well-organized and easy to review.
improvements: To improve complexity, consider breaking down the commit into smaller, more focused changes. For example, if this commit is related to user authentication, consider creating separate commits for user registration, login, and password reset.

To make the commit more impactful, try to explain the benefits of the change in the commit message. For instance, 'feat: improved user experience by allowing mapped users to commit changes' instead of just 'feat: commit by mapped user'.
learning_tip: When working on a feature, try to identify the smallest, most focused change that still achieves the desired outcome. This will help you create more manageable commits and improve code complexity.
example_improved_message: feat: improved user experience by allowing mapped users to commit changes
